-{"id":"beads_rust-qc76","title":"[P2] MCP label/dependency/comment side effects discard storage errors","description":"Root cause: create_issue and update_issue in src/mcp/tools.rs ignore Results from side-effect mutations: add_label at src/mcp/tools.rs:1277, add_dependency at src/mcp/tools.rs:1281, add_label/remove_label at src/mcp/tools.rs:1470 and src/mcp/tools.rs:1473, and add_comment at src/mcp/tools.rs:1480. A validation error, duplicate dependency, database write failure, or comment insert failure becomes a successful MCP response with requested changes missing.

Repro: call MCP update_issue with an invalid label in labels_add, or fault-inject add_comment/add_dependency failure. The response reports the issue as updated even though the side effect was not applied.

Proposed fix sketch: propagate each side-effect Result through beads_to_mcp, or collect side-effect failures and fail the whole mutation transaction. Avoid partial-success responses unless the response explicitly carries structured per-operation warnings and the committed state is reloaded.

Test to add: MCP create/update invalid label, duplicate parent dependency, and failing comment insertion return a structured error and do not report success for absent side effects.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:03:19.418029102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:03:19.418029102Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"beads_rust-qc76","title":"[P2] MCP label/dependency/comment side effects discard storage errors","description":"Root cause: create_issue and update_issue in src/mcp/tools.rs ignore Results from side-effect mutations: add_label at src/mcp/tools.rs:1277, add_dependency at src/mcp/tools.rs:1281, add_label/remove_label at src/mcp/tools.rs:1470 and src/mcp/tools.rs:1473, and add_comment at src/mcp/tools.rs:1480. A validation error, duplicate dependency, database write failure, or comment insert failure becomes a successful MCP response with requested changes missing.

Repro: call MCP update_issue with an invalid label in labels_add, or fault-inject add_comment/add_dependency failure. The response reports the issue as updated even though the side effect was not applied.

Proposed fix sketch: propagate each side-effect Result through beads_to_mcp, or collect side-effect failures and fail the whole mutation transaction. Avoid partial-success responses unless the response explicitly carries structured per-operation warnings and the committed state is reloaded.

Test to add: MCP create/update invalid label, duplicate parent dependency, and failing comment insertion return a structured error and do not report success for absent side effects.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"agent_2282484","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:03:19.418029102Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T19:40:31.797497321Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-qeuv","title":"Add golden coverage for ready and bv robot schemas","description":"Testing posture: golden artifacts

Finding: agent workflows depend on br ready --robot and bv --robot-* outputs for automated work selection. These robot surfaces need frozen-golden coverage so accidental schema, field-name, ordering, and ranking-shape changes are visible before release.

Acceptance criteria:
- Add deterministic fixture data for ready-work ranking with blocked, deferred, assigned, unassigned, priority, label, and dependency cases.
- Add golden tests for br ready --robot output with exact normalized JSON comparison.
- Add golden tests or checked fixture snapshots for representative bv --robot-* outputs, at minimum bv --robot-triage, bv --robot-next, and bv --robot-plan, using the same fixture graph where practical.
- Normalize volatile fields such as elapsed timings, hashes, absolute paths, and timestamps only where required, with normalization documented beside the goldens.
- Add a documented update workflow that makes intentional robot schema changes reviewable as artifact diffs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T21:51:33.718593428Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T22:12:53.403303903Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T22:12:53.403031443Z","close_reason":"Added deterministic golden fixtures for br ready --robot and representative bv robot schema outputs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":289,"issue_id":"beads_rust-qeuv","author":"ubuntu","text":"degraded-coordination: Agent Mail health is red; intended edit surface is tests/snapshots/json_output.rs or a focused snapshots module plus insta snapshot artifacts for br ready --robot and bv --robot-triage/next/plan.","created_at":"2026-04-21T22:02:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-ql7","title":"config Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br config` command for viewing and modifying configuration settings. Configuration is stored per-project in the .beads directory.

## CLI Interface
```
br config <COMMAND>

Commands:
  get <KEY>                   Get a config value
  set <KEY> <VALUE>           Set a config value
  list                        List all config values
  reset <KEY>                 Reset to default
  init                        Create default config

Options:
  --global                    Use global (~/.config/br/) config
  --json                      Output as JSON
```

## Configuration Keys

### Core Settings
```toml
# .beads/config.toml (or stored in SQLite config table)

[core]
prefix = "beads_rust"         # ID prefix for new issues
default_priority = 2          # Default priority for new issues
default_type = "task"         # Default type for new issues

[display]
color = true                  # Enable colored output
date_format = "relative"      # "relative", "iso", "local"
truncate_titles = 80          # Max title length in list view

[sync]
auto_flush = false            # Auto-export after changes (non-invasive default)
auto_import = true            # Auto-import if JSONL is newer

[user]
name = ""                     # Override for commit author
email = ""                    # Override for commit email
```

### Implementation
```rust
struct Config {
    core: CoreConfig,
    display: DisplayConfig,
    sync: SyncConfig,
    user: UserConfig,
}

impl Config {
    fn load(beads_dir: &Path) -> Result<Self> {
        // Try config.toml first
        let config_path = beads_dir.join("config.toml");
        if config_path.exists() {
            return Self::from_toml(&config_path);
        }
        
        // Fall back to SQLite config table
        let db_path = beads_dir.join("*.db");
        if let Some(db) = find_db(&beads_dir)? {
            return Self::from_sqlite(&db);
        }
        
        Ok(Self::default())
    }
    
    fn get(&self, key: &str) -> Option<String> {
        match key {
            "core.prefix" => Some(self.core.prefix.clone()),
            "display.color" => Some(self.display.color.to_string()),
            // ... etc
            _ => None,
        }
    }
}
```

### Database Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE config (
    key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    value TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
);
```

## Output Formats

### List (Human-readable)
```
Configuration for .beads/:

[core]
  prefix = beads_rust
  default_priority = 2
  default_type = task

[display]
  color = true
  date_format = relative

[sync]
  auto_flush = false
  auto_import = true
```

### JSON
```json
{
  "core": {
    "prefix": "beads_rust",
    "default_priority": 2
  },
  "display": {
    "color": true
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Get individual config values
- [ ] Set config values
- [ ] List all config values
- [ ] Reset to defaults
- [ ] Support global config (~/.config/br/)
- [ ] Merge global + project config (project wins)
- [ ] Validate config values
- [ ] Human-readable and JSON output

## Dependencies
- Requires init Command (config location)
- Requires SQLite Storage Layer (config table)

## Rationale
Configuration allows customization without modifying code. Global config handles user preferences (color, name); project config handles project-specific settings (prefix). The non-invasive defaults (auto_flush = false) align with br's philosophy.

 {"id":"beads_rust-qlh","title":"search Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br search` command for full-text search across issues. This enables finding issues by content when you don't know exact IDs or titles.

## CLI Interface
```
br search [OPTIONS] <QUERY>

Arguments:
  <QUERY>  Search query (supports basic operators)

Options:
  -s, --status <STATUS>     Limit to status (open, closed, all)
  -t, --type <TYPE>         Limit to type
  --fields <FIELDS>         Fields to search (title, description, comments)
  --limit <N>               Max results (default: 20)
  --json                    Output as JSON
  --robot                   Machine-readable output
```

## Implementation Details

### SQLite FTS5 Setup
```sql
-- Full-text search virtual table
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE issues_fts USING fts5(
    id,
    title,
    description,
    content='issues',
    content_rowid='rowid',
    tokenize='porter unicode61'
);

-- Triggers to keep FTS in sync
CREATE TRIGGER issues_ai AFTER INSERT ON issues BEGIN
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(rowid, id, title, description)
    VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.description);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER issues_ad AFTER DELETE ON issues BEGIN
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(issues_fts, rowid, id, title, description)
    VALUES ('delete', old.rowid, old.id, old.title, old.description);
END;

CREATE TRIGGER issues_au AFTER UPDATE ON issues BEGIN
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(issues_fts, rowid, id, title, description)
    VALUES ('delete', old.rowid, old.id, old.title, old.description);
    INSERT INTO issues_fts(rowid, id, title, description)
    VALUES (new.rowid, new.id, new.title, new.description);
END;
```

### Search Implementation
```rust
fn search(&self, query: &str, opts: SearchOptions) -> Result<Vec<SearchResult>> {
    // Use FTS5 MATCH syntax
    let sql = r#"
        SELECT 
            i.*,
            highlight(issues_fts, 1, '<mark>', '</mark>') as title_highlight,
            highlight(issues_fts, 2, '<mark>', '</mark>') as desc_highlight,
            bm25(issues_fts) as rank
        FROM issues_fts
        JOIN issues i ON issues_fts.id = i.id
        WHERE issues_fts MATCH ?
        ORDER BY rank
        LIMIT ?
    "#;
    
    // FTS5 query syntax:
    // - Simple terms: "authentication"
    // - Phrase: "user authentication"
    // - OR: "auth OR login"
    // - NOT: "auth NOT oauth"
    // - Prefix: "auth*"
}
```

### Comment Search
Optionally search comments as well:
```sql
CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE comments_fts USING fts5(
    issue_id,
    body,
    content='comments',
    content_rowid='rowid',
    tokenize='porter unicode61'
);
```

## Output Formats

### Human-readable
```
Search results for "authentication" (5 matches):

[P0] beads_rust-abc123  Implement user <mark>authentication</mark>
     "...JWT-based <mark>authentication</mark> with refresh tokens..."

[P1] beads_rust-def456  Fix <mark>authentication</mark> timeout bug
     "...session expires during <mark>authentication</mark> flow..."

[P2] beads_rust-ghi789  Document <mark>authentication</mark> API
     "...describes the <mark>authentication</mark> endpoints..."
```

### JSON
```json
{
  "query": "authentication",
  "results": [
    {
      "id": "beads_rust-abc123",
      "title": "Implement user authentication",
      "rank": 0.95,
      "highlights": {
        "title": "Implement user <mark>authentication</mark>",
        "description": "...JWT-based <mark>authentication</mark>..."
      }
    }
  ],
  "count": 5
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Full-text search across titles
- [ ] Full-text search across descriptions
- [ ] Optional comment search
- [ ] Support FTS5 query operators (phrase, OR, NOT, prefix)
- [ ] Rank results by relevance (BM25)
- [ ] Highlight matching terms
- [ ] Filter by status/type
- [ ] Limit results
- [ ] Keep FTS index in sync with changes

## Dependencies
- Requires SQLite Storage Layer (FTS5 extension)
- Requires Schema & Migrations (FTS tables/triggers)
- Requires Model Types

## Rationale
Search is essential when the issue database grows large. Users need to find issues by remembered keywords, not just IDs. FTS5 provides stemming (finding "authenticate" when searching "authentication") and ranking.

@@ -688,7 +688,7 @@
 {"id":"beads_rust-vh19","title":"Name ID generation magic numbers in util/id.rs","description":"SUBTASK 6.2: src/util/id.rs contains several unnamed magic numbers in the ID generation algorithm that controls collision avoidance:

Line 133: for _ in 0..10 — nonce retry count per hash length
  Add: const NONCE_RETRIES_PER_LENGTH: u32 = 10;

Line 150: 12 — fallback hash length when max_hash_length exhausted
  Add: const FALLBACK_HASH_LENGTH: usize = 12;

Line 161: 1000 — safety break threshold for desperate ID generation
  Add: const SAFETY_BREAK_THRESHOLD: u32 = 1000;

Line 169: 2000 — hard stop to prevent infinite loop
  Add: const HARD_STOP_LIMIT: u32 = 2000;

Line 520: 6 — abbreviation threshold for prefix length
  Add: const ABBREVIATION_THRESHOLD: usize = 6;

Line 543: 3 — fallback abbreviation length
  Add: const FALLBACK_ABBREVIATION_LENGTH: usize = 3;

CONTEXT: The ID generation algorithm starts with short hashes and progressively lengthens them to avoid collisions. The nonce adds randomness at each length. The safety breaks prevent pathological cases from looping forever. These are important algorithm parameters that deserve documentation via naming.

REASONING: If someone needs to debug "why did ID generation take so long?" or "why are my IDs longer than expected?", these named constants immediately point to the tuning knobs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:31.025860676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T04:03:06.891054594Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T04:03:06.890565438Z","close_reason":"6 ID generation magic numbers named: NONCE_RETRIES_PER_LENGTH, FALLBACK_HASH_LENGTH, SAFETY_BREAK_THRESHOLD, HARD_STOP_LIMIT, ABBREVIATION_THRESHOLD, FALLBACK_ABBREVIATION_LENGTH.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-vkc","title":"graph Command (dependency visualization)","description":"# graph Command (dependency visualization)

## Purpose
Optional read-only dependency visualization matching bd semantics (reverse-dependency traversal).

## CLI
```
br graph <issue-id>
br graph --all
```
Flags: `--compact` (one line per issue), `--json`.

## Behavior (classic)
- For a single issue, graph traverses **dependents** only (reverse deps); direct dependencies of root are not added.
- `--all`: builds connected components over open/in_progress/blocked issues.
- Layout uses `blocks` edges only; layering by longest path.

## JSON Output
Returns layout with nodes/layers (capitalized keys in legacy; can be normalized in br if explicitly decided).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reverse-dependency traversal matches bd.
- JSON output includes root, issues, and layout data.

## Tests
- Graph of simple dependency chain.
- `--all` with multiple components.","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SapphireGrove","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:18:42.211150085Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T08:38:44.267688299Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T08:38:44.267688299Z","close_reason":"Implemented graph command with single-issue dependents traversal and --all connected components mode. Supports --compact and --json output. Uses blocks edges only.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-vlt","title":"CLI command unit tests: All command modules","description":"# CLI Command Unit Tests: All Command Modules

## Logging in Unit Tests
Each test should:
\`\`\`rust
#[test]
fn test_example() {
    init_test_logging();
    info!("test_example: starting");
    // ... test code ...
    info!("test_example: assertions passed");
}
\`\`\`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 71+ unit tests across 9 command modules
- [ ] Each module has its own #[cfg(test)] section
- [ ] Tests run with: cargo test --lib
- [ ] All tests log entry/exit
- [ ] No external dependencies (no temp dirs, no actual DB)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleLake","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T16:30:20.664223434Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T02:35:50.903235466Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T02:35:50.903235466Z","close_reason":"All specified command modules already have #[cfg(test)] unit tests with logging; list/create/init/show/blocked/dep/comments/update/delete coverage present. -{"id":"beads_rust-vmbw","title":"[P2] MCP read surfaces default storage failures to empty data","description":"Root cause: MCP resource/tool helpers use unwrap_or_default on storage reads in data and diagnostic paths. src/mcp/tools.rs:242 and src/mcp/resources.rs:24 turn get_all_ids failures into no fuzzy suggestions; src/mcp/resources.rs:80 turns get_all_config failure into an empty config map; src/mcp/tools.rs:1593-1596 hides blocker/dependent lookup failures after close_issue. These are database/storage errors that should be visible to clients, not represented as empty project state or missing warnings.

Repro: fault-inject storage query failure for get_all_config, get_all_ids, get_blockers, or get_blocked_issue_ids while serving MCP. Project/info can still return config={}, issue-not-found loses suggestions, and close_issue can report success without blocker/dependent warning context.

Proposed fix sketch: propagate storage read failures through to_mcp/beads_to_mcp when the resource purpose is data retrieval. For non-fatal post-close warning lookups, include a structured warnings field carrying the underlying error instead of defaulting to empty vectors.

+{"id":"beads_rust-vmbw","title":"[P2] MCP read surfaces default storage failures to empty data","description":"Root cause: MCP resource/tool helpers use unwrap_or_default on storage reads in data and diagnostic paths. src/mcp/tools.rs:242 and src/mcp/resources.rs:24 turn get_all_ids failures into no fuzzy suggestions; src/mcp/resources.rs:80 turns get_all_config failure into an empty config map; src/mcp/tools.rs:1593-1596 hides blocker/dependent lookup failures after close_issue. These are database/storage errors that should be visible to clients, not represented as empty project state or missing warnings.

Repro: fault-inject storage query failure for get_all_config, get_all_ids, get_blockers, or get_blocked_issue_ids while serving MCP. Project/info can still return config={}, issue-not-found loses suggestions, and close_issue can report success without blocker/dependent warning context.

Proposed fix sketch: propagate storage read failures through to_mcp/beads_to_mcp when the resource purpose is data retrieval. For non-fatal post-close warning lookups, include a structured warnings field carrying the underlying error instead of defaulting to empty vectors.

 {"id":"beads_rust-vo6l","title":"[LOW] README command inventory omits implemented user-facing subcommands","description":"Root cause: README.md command table at README.md:388-449 documents only a subset of the public Clap command enum. The implementation exposes additional user-facing commands in src/cli/mod.rs:733-942 and main dispatch in src/main.rs:202-332: agents, audit, changelog, completions, defer, epic, graph, history, info, lint, orphans, query, schema, status, undefer, and where. Some are mentioned elsewhere, but the README Commands section presents itself as the command reference and leaves these discoverability gaps. Repro: compare README.md:388-449 with `RUST_LOG=error br --help` or src/cli/mod.rs:733-942. Proposed fix: either make README Commands explicitly a quick-start subset with a link to docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md, or add rows/sections for every implemented public subcommand and major nested command.  {"id":"beads_rust-vqmz","title":"Reject symlinked DB recovery before WAL quarantine","description":"Round-2 review found that symlinked beads.db recovery now refuses to rebuild the database path, but open_sqlite_storage_with_recovery_strategy still calls quarantine_truncated_wal_sidecar before the symlink rejection inside backup/cleanup. A symlinked beads.db with a truncated beads.db-wal can therefore create .br_recovery artifacts and move sidecars even though recovery should be a no-op refusal. Move the symlink guard ahead of pre-open sidecar quarantine and add regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:21:00.931167361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T18:27:30.043330254Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T18:27:30.042997851Z","close_reason":"Skipped truncated WAL quarantine for symlinked database paths and extended symlink recovery coverage so refusal leaves sidecars and targets untouched.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
